result:
If I could change the world in one way, it would be to instill in every person a deeply felt, inescapable sense of **interconnection**.  

Not as a vague idea, but as a visceral understanding that your well-being is fundamentally tied to the well-being of others—across the street, across borders, across species, and across generations.  

This wouldn’t erase differences or conflict, but it would reframe them. Empathy would cease to be optional; exploitation and indifference would feel as self-harming as cutting your own flesh. Systems would be designed not just for efficiency or profit, but for mutual thriving.  

Much of what harms our world—environmental destruction, profound inequality, prejudice, war—stems from a failure to truly grasp that we are part of a single, living web. Changing that core perception would change everything that flows from it.
